design_philosophy: The Anchorage is the most commercially dense starting region. Businesses here are genuine commercial operations serving the actual needs of a major international harbor — ship repair, provisioning, trade facilitation, information exchange, accommodation for people passing through from every nation on Arneth. The commercial layer is real and fully functional. Underneath it runs the information layer, which is denser than any single business would suggest and which connects to every other starting region through the threads that converge here. Business names in the Shattered Reach tend toward the functional and the geographic — places are named for what they do or where they sit in relation to the water.

anchor_businesses.name: Tidewarden Cathedral — Halvard's Rock
anchor_businesses.type: Religious institution — one of three primary Tidewarden cathedrals on Arneth
anchor_businesses.location: Main island upper tier, visible from forty miles at sea
anchor_businesses.description: The largest building in the Anchorage and the most visible. Built on the highest point of the main island over approximately four hundred years — the core is old Shattered Reach stonework, the additions are each successive century's contribution to the institution's physical expression. The library is the best in the Shattered Reach. Saint Halvard's burial site at the waterline is technically a separate institution but functionally part of the cathedral's spiritual geography.

resources.library: Includes the pre-Reckoning navigation chart with the submarine ridge notation. Historical navigation records going back eight hundred years. Church records for the Shattered Reach. The most complete Tidewarden theological archive in the Reach.
resources.financial_instruments: The Church owns three warehouse complexes, two dry docks, and the pilgrimage facilities. Financial weight comparable to any Compact council member.

charitable_stores.current_level: Substantial — the Anchorage's most significant charitable operation

saint_halvards_burial_site.location: Harbor walk, eastern face of the main island, at the waterline
saint_halvards_burial_site.current_anomaly: Anomalous resistance has been responding to the disc on the outer harbor ship for three weeks. The High Warden has been going at night. The junior warden has been following her. The bioluminescence immediately around the site intensifies when the response is strongest.
saint_halvards_burial_site.player_significance: Bringing the disc to the burial site produces an intensified response. What the full response produces at maximum proximity has not been documented. A player with sufficient knowledge of saint sites and artifact interactions — Pale Company, First Scholars, Moor Communion, or Keeperhood background — will have a hypothesis.

hooks: The pre-Reckoning navigation chart and the submarine ridge
The High Warden's nocturnal burial site visits
The nine-year communion avoidance record for Halvwater
The Salt Silence attendance contact at services

anchor_businesses.name: Reach Compact Hall
anchor_businesses.type: Governing institution — council chambers, administrative offices, customs authority
anchor_businesses.location: Main island middle tier, facing the harbor
anchor_businesses.description: The Compact's institutional home. Built to impress and to function — high ceilings for the council chamber, efficient organization for the administrative wing. The customs office processes three hundred manifests a week. The harbor administrator manages day-to-day operations from here. The council meets three times per ten-day.

resources.administrative_records: Every manifest filed in the Anchorage. Every harbor movement. The six months of Halvwater notations that form the pattern Wenda Saltcrest has been building.
resources.customs_records: Full manifest and cargo inspection records. The clerical error notation for the outer harbor ship is here.

hooks: Wenda Saltcrest's investigation — accessible through the hiring board posting
The manifest discrepancy and clerical error notation
The Compact recognition negotiation meetings — Iron Wake attends
Tav Coldwater's discomfort about filing the notation

anchor_businesses.name: Pilotage Station
anchor_businesses.type: Harbor authority — pilot licensing, vessel approach and departure, harbor safety
anchor_businesses.description: Every vessel entering or leaving the harbor is required by Compact regulation to take on an Anchorage pilot for the approach or departure. The pilot station licenses pilots, assigns work, and maintains the harbor's approach and departure records. The best existing record of every vessel movement in the Anchorage is in the pilot station's daily logs.
anchor_businesses.halvard_wardens_position: Senior harbor pilot. The pilot station is his genuine workplace and his operational base simultaneously. The pilot boats run on a schedule that provides legitimate reason to be in every part of the harbor including the outer anchorage at any time of day or night.

resources.vessel_movement_logs: Every approach, departure, position change, and notable event in the harbor. The outer harbor ship's course correction pattern is in three weeks of logs.
resources.historical_navigation_records: Including the record of two previous 'current calling' events documented by Tev Roughwater's predecessor.

hooks: Halvard Warden — pilot and Warden
The vessel movement logs and the course correction arc
The 'current calling' historical records
The pilot station work path — legitimate employment for sailor and maritime backgrounds

anchor_businesses.name: The Silver Tide
anchor_businesses.location: Main island, Cathedral Quarter, adjacent to the Compact Hall
anchor_businesses.description: The Anchorage's finest inn. Twenty rooms, none cheap. Serves the Compact officials, senior merchants, and visiting powers' representatives. The common room is the most politically sensitive space in the Anchorage — every conversation in it is potentially observed by someone with a specific interest in what is being said.
anchor_businesses.the_iron_wake_here: Rael Sorveth has been in room fourteen for six days. The innkeeper knows. Nobody else has been told.

pricing.meal: One bronze mark — the best food in the Anchorage

hooks: The Iron Wake's strategic common room visibility
The political observation layer in the common room
The innkeeper's knowledge and discretion

anchor_businesses.name: The Tide's End
anchor_businesses.type: Inn and tavern — Outer Harbor, working harbor accommodation
anchor_businesses.description: The roughest and the most honest establishment in the Anchorage. Serves the working harbor community — dock workers, fishing crews, sailors between berths. The common room has a harbor-facing window from which three weeks of outer harbor ship observation have been conducted by Tev Roughwater.

pricing.private_room: Five iron chits per night — four rooms, two of which are barely private
pricing.meal: One iron chit — adequate and honest

hooks: Dava Salthorn at the fourth table
Wren Daltborn's bribe refusal story spreading through the working community
Tev Roughwater's thirty-two years of harbor observation
The Outer Harbor information network available to players who spend time here

anchor_businesses.name: The Thornback (Iron Wake's vessel)
anchor_businesses.type: Corsair Throne sovereign's vessel — anchored in the main harbor
anchor_businesses.location: Main harbor, allocated berth for official Compact visitor vessels
anchor_businesses.description: A forty-gun ship that has been at sea for twelve years under the Iron Wake's command and shows it — well-maintained in the way of something that is genuinely used, not in the way of something maintained for display. Flies the Corsair Throne's colors, which the Compact permits under the recognition negotiation protocol.
anchor_businesses.aboard: The Iron Wake. Sorn Waveward. Essa Saltmark. A crew of eighty who rotate shore leave on a schedule that ensures the Thornback is never understaffed.
hooks: The Iron Wake — accessible aboard after she has assessed a player as worth the direct approach
Essa Saltmark's dossier on the Keth-Anchorage connection
The deserter spawn hook recruitment path

anchorage_guaranteed: Six additional inns — range from budget to comfortable, distributed across main island tiers
Three premium inns — Cathedral Quarter and upper Merchant tier
Ship repair yards x3 — varying size and capability
Rope walk and rigging supply x2
Main market — lower tier, daily, largest market in any starting region by volume
Precious goods and luxury imports x4
Grain and food storage merchants x5
Money changers x5 — all major currencies
Halverness Factors office — significant presence, connects to Keth and Amber Coast threads
Salt Silence node — retired chandler's operation (Kern Saltborn)
Varathi Return cult rented rooms x3 — not a business, a mistake
Physician x2 — one Church-affiliated, one independent
Arms dealer — strictly regulated by Compact, fully licensed
Cartographer — the best charts on Arneth available here
The harbor walk — not a business, a route. All harbor walk businesses face it.

anchorage_roll_1d8.1: Foreign power's permanent office — roll for which of the 20 Arneth powers currently represented
anchorage_roll_1d8.2: Luxury goods importer with specific single-region specialty
anchorage_roll_1d8.4: Ship captain's association meeting hall — exclusive, information-dense
anchorage_roll_1d8.5: Exclusive private dining club — political conversations happen here
anchorage_roll_1d8.6: Navigation school — trains pilots and navigators, Cael Wyrnmark's occasional client
anchorage_roll_1d8.8: Rare materials broker — including skyfall iron when available

outer_harbor_guaranteed: The outer harbor breakwater walk — observation point for the outer anchorage
shelf_island_guaranteed: Ferry to main island — three daily crossings plus on-demand

ship_provisioning_schedule.description: The Pale Current is provisioned every five days by Fen Daltwick. The provisioning schedule is publicly knowable through the harbor's chandler records. A player who calculates the next provisioning date can arrange to be at the outer harbor at the right time.
ship_provisioning_schedule.notes: The captain receives the chandler aboard personally. This is the only regular access point to the ship's deck.

chalk_scratching_degradation.description: The chalk scratching on sailcloth is degrading. Approximately forty percent legible on the ship's arrival. Losing approximately five percent legibility per week.
chalk_scratching_degradation.timeline: Four to six weeks before fully illegible. The cult does not know how to preserve it. The First Scholars do. A player who connects these two facts creates an urgent information-sharing situation.

saint_halvard_burial_site_response.description: The burial site's anomalous resistance has been responding to the disc at outer harbor distance for three weeks. If the disc is moved closer to the site, the response intensifies proportionally. Full proximity response has not been documented.
saint_halvard_burial_site_response.player_mechanic: A player who brings the disc to the burial site will experience the response directly. The specific quality of the experience will depend on the player's background — a Pale Company character, a Tidewarden character, a Moor Communion character, and a character with no religious background will each experience it differently. The experience produces specific information about the artifact system that is not available anywhere else.

the_full_cross_regional_thread.title: The Long Arc
the_full_cross_regional_thread.description: The Anchorage is where the cross-regional threads converge for the player who has been paying attention across all six starting regions.

the_four_artifact_instances.artifact: Steel-and-gemstone disc — containment marker. In the locked box in Brother Soren Aldrath's study. Found by Aldren Saltmeadow in his east field.
the_four_artifact_instances.phenomenon: Directional awareness — replacing compass north with a specific pull toward an unidentified point

the_four_artifact_instances.artifact: The inscription — 4,000 characters in pre-Varathi script in the sealed chamber at the New Work site. The First Scholars have forty percent translated. The readable portions describe a location and express fear.
the_four_artifact_instances.phenomenon: The same directional awareness in people who have spent extended time in the Archive's third sublevel, above whatever is beneath it. The Oldmark excavation director has an unreported fragment.

the_four_artifact_instances.artifact: The sealed peat container — approximately 2,000 years old, cold to the touch, unidentified. In the locked room of the Mossward Station. Brynn Holst cannot complete the identification rite.
the_four_artifact_instances.phenomenon: A different quality — awareness, as if the container knows where specific people are. The peat cutter who first touched it is experiencing this. The Moor Communion's 'deep archive' oral tradition reference.

the_four_artifact_instances.artifact: Second steel-and-gemstone disc — the same type as the Thornsveld marker. Recovered from the New Work site by Varathi Return cult operatives. Aboard the Pale Current in the outer harbor. Plus: chalk scratching of approximately forty percent of the Keth inscription.
the_four_artifact_instances.phenomenon: The burial site of Saint Halvard responding to the disc at harbor distance. The two affected crew members orienting toward a specific heading. The bioluminescence beneath the ship behaving differently.

the_shared_heading.description: The two affected crew members on the outer harbor ship keep orienting toward a specific heading — south-southeast from the Anchorage. This heading, extended, leads to a submarine ridge that appears on a pre-Reckoning chart in the Tidewarden Cathedral library but not on any current chart.
the_shared_heading.idwal_bogfen_confirmation: The Free Fens person who was moved out of the northwest fen by Sova Fenwick and arrived in the Anchorage is also orienting toward the same heading. They came from the northwest fen. The crew came from the Keth plateau. The same heading from different geographic starting points thousands of miles apart.
the_shared_heading.the_ridge: The submarine ridge on the old chart is annotated in the same pre-Varathi script as the Keth inscription. The annotation has not been translated. Cael Wyrnmark has been trying to locate the ridge on modern charts for four days without success.
the_shared_heading.what_a_full_player_can_assemble: A player who has been in all six starting regions has the following pieces: the Thornsveld disc (containment marker, directional awareness), the Keth inscription (describing a location, expressing fear), the Mossward container (the deep archive, condition for opening is 'when the old sealings fail'), the northwest fen contamination moving toward something in the deep bog (Orveth's knowledge that it is being drawn to the holding place), the Anchorage disc (same type as Thornsveld), and the chalk scratching (forty percent of the inscription, degrading). Plus: the shared heading from three independent sources pointing to the submarine ridge. The ridge is the destination of whatever the pre-Reckoning system was built to reach. The sealings are failing. The system is activating. The player is the only entity on Arneth who has assembled this picture.

what_no_npc_knows_fully.halvard_warden: Knows about the disc, the burial site response, and the cult operation. Does not know about the Mossward container, the Fens contamination connection, or Idwal Bogfen.
what_no_npc_knows_fully.kern_saltborn: Knows about all Anchorage threads including Halvwater embedding. Does not know about the Mossward container or the Fens contamination, and has not connected the shared heading to the submarine ridge.
what_no_npc_knows_fully.sova_pale_in_keth: Knows what is under the Archive. Does not know about the Anchorage disc, the Fens container, or the shared heading.
what_no_npc_knows_fully.orveth_in_fens: Knows the northwest fen contamination is being drawn toward the deep holding place. Does not know about the discs, the inscription, or the Anchorage.
what_no_npc_knows_fully.drevhan_coldbeck_in_borderlands: Knows the Bleeding Ground is approaching threshold. Has no connection to the artifact system.
what_no_npc_knows_fully.the_player: The only possible assembler of the full picture. The player who has been everywhere is the only entity on Arneth with enough pieces to understand what is happening. What they do with the understanding is their decision. The world does not require them to act. The world is not waiting for a chosen one. It is turning and will continue to turn. The choice to act — or not to act — is the player's own.

the_games_title.the_long_march_constellation: Visible in winter. Named for the historical migration of the armies of the Third Turning's collapse — the generation that marched from one end of Cauldenmoor to the other and did not stop because stopping meant acknowledging there was nowhere left to go. In sailors' tradition, the constellation marks the end of the safe navigation season. In soldiers' tradition, it marks the beginning of the march that does not end until it ends. A player who has assembled the full picture and is looking at the sky in the Anchorage on a winter night will see the Long March constellation in the south. It is pointing, roughly, toward the submarine ridge.
